summary: Future PLC has appointed Patricia Corsi, Chief Growth Officer at Kimberly-Clark with extensive marketing and brand expertise, and Anna Mallett, Vice President of Production at Netflix with deep media content experience, as Independent Non-Executive Directors from 21 July 2026. Both join the Nomination Committee. Chair Mark Brooker highlighted their relevance to Future's brand transformation programme, channel-agnostic strategy and new product development. The appointments bring senior commercial and media distribution expertise to support the group's growth strategy.
retail_take: Market-positive signal: both appointees bring relevant expertise in brand strategy and media distribution aligned with Future's stated priorities, suggesting board strength to execute transformation and growth objectives.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: Supreme CEO Sandy Chadha purchases 500,000 shares, increasing stake to 54.69% of issued capital.
summary: Sandy Chadha, Chief Executive Officer of Supreme plc, acquired 500,000 ordinary shares at 131.811p per share on 20 July 2026 through his wholly-owned vehicle SC8 Limited, investing £659,055. Following this purchase, Chadha's total holding reaches 64,166,845 shares, representing approximately 54.69% of the company's issued share capital. The transaction occurred on the London Stock Exchange AIM market.
retail_take: Insider buying by the controlling shareholder signals confidence in Supreme's prospects, though the large existing stake limits incremental significance to broader shareholder base.

headline: Board Changes
brief: Rod Waldie steps down as CEO for health reasons from 1 August 2026. Martin Pike, Non-Executive Director since April 2025, appointed Interim CEO.
summary: Rod Waldie is resigning as Chief Executive Officer and Executive Director of Gateley (Holdings) PLC effective 1 August 2026 due to personal health reasons. Martin Pike, who joined as Independent Non-Executive Director and Audit Committee Chair in April 2025, will assume the role of Interim Chief Executive Officer. Pike brings 30 years of experience from Willis Towers Watson and subsequent senior roles at FTSE companies. A permanent CEO search will commence, with focus on margin enhancement and profitable growth.
retail_take: Managed transition with experienced interim leader provides stability, though CEO departures often create near-term uncertainty. Market likely views positively if Pike is seen as credible; execution risk remains on finding permanent replacement.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Mark H Dixon and Diana Dixon increased stake in SkinBioTherapeutics to 28.36% via Rock Nominees Limited.
summary: Mark H Dixon and Diana Dixon, holding shares through Rock Nominees Limited, crossed a notification threshold on 17 July 2026, increasing their voting rights from 26.971% to 28.359% (73.5m shares). The issuer was notified on 20 July 2026. This represents an acquisition of voting rights in the London-listed biotech company, with the stake now held directly in ordinary shares without financial instruments involved.
retail_take: Routine major shareholding disclosure showing insider accumulation; suggests confidence but standard notification with no immediate strategic implications signalled.

headline: Director/PDMR Share Dealing
brief: Accsys CEO Jelena Arsic van Os sells 13,227 shares at 73p to cover tax liabilities from deferred bonus vesting.
summary: On 18 July 2026, 28,120 shares vested for CEO Jelena Arsic van Os under the Deferred Bonus Plan. She sold 13,227 shares at 73.0 pence per share on 20 July 2026 to settle personal tax and social security obligations, while retaining 14,893 shares to meet shareholding requirements. Following the sale, she holds 536,223 shares representing approximately 0.22% of issued share capital.
retail_take: Routine director dealing disclosure. Share sale driven by tax obligations from bonus vesting rather than loss of confidence; retention of majority of vested shares suggests confidence in company direction.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: FMR LLC (Fidelity) holding in Zegona Communications fell below 10% threshold on 17 July 2026.
summary: FMR LLC's voting rights in Zegona Communications PLC decreased from 13.41% to 13.02% (29.36m shares), crossing below the 10% aggregate threshold through controlled undertaking Fidelity Management & Research Company LLC, which fell from 5.74% to below reportable levels. The threshold breach was notified on 20 July 2026, with the position change recorded on 17 July.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of major shareholder stake reduction. Fidelity's decreased involvement may suggest portfolio rebalancing rather than strategic concern; immaterial for most retail investors but could indicate weakening institutional support.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Premier Miton Group plc crosses 8% voting rights threshold in Technology Minerals PLC.
summary: Premier Miton Group plc notified on 20 July 2026 that it has acquired voting rights in Technology Minerals PLC (ISIN: GB00BWYF7709), crossing the 8% disclosure threshold. Premier Miton now holds 1,000,000,000 voting rights representing 8.002895% of total voting rights, acquired through a chain of controlled undertakings including Premier Asset Management entities and Premier Investment Group Ltd. The threshold crossing occurred on 20 July 2026 with notification on the same date.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of a major shareholding position by an established asset management group; no material implications evident from the notification itself.

headline: Acquisition update and H1 2026 trading update
brief: Rosebank upgrades 2026 outlook after completing MW Components and CPM acquisitions; cost-cutting and restructuring underway across portfolio.
summary: Rosebank completed acquisitions of MW Components (12 May) and CPM (28 May) 2026, with both performing ahead or in line with expectations. MW Components' three businesses are being legally separated with $15m annual head office cost cuts and factory closures planned. CPM acquired its UK/Ireland aftermarket distributor for €26m and targets $10m annual cost reductions. ECI trading in line with guidance despite 13% A&H revenue decline offset by 9% E&I growth and 16.1% adjusted operating margin. Group adjusted operating profit and EPS expected ahead of 2026 consensus ($294m) with confidence in 2027 outperformance ($452m consensus).
retail_take: Market likely to read positively: upgraded full-year guidance, swift operational improvements post-acquisition, and strong order intake in MW Components and CPM suggest effective execution and justified acquisition multiples. Material cost actions ($25m+ identified) de-risk guidance.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Edinburgh Investment Trust buys back 50,000 shares at 827.99p per share for treasury.
summary: Edinburgh Investment Trust PLC purchased 50,000 ordinary shares on 20 July 2026 via Investec Bank plc at an average price of 827.99p per share on the London Stock Exchange. The shares will be held in treasury. Following this transaction, the company holds 69,799,709 treasury shares out of 195,666,734 issued shares, leaving 125,867,025 shares with voting rights in issue.
retail_take: Routine share buyback reducing share count; typical capital management activity with modest scale relative to total issued capital.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: AEP Plantations repurchased 57,142 shares at 163-166p as part of announced buyback programme.
summary: AEP Plantations purchased 57,142 ordinary shares on 20 July 2026 at prices ranging from 163p to 166p, with a volume-weighted average price of 164.86p. Since announcing the buyback programme on 6 July, the company has repurchased 534,315 shares held in treasury. Post-purchase, AEP has 399,762,720 shares in issue with 383,059,165 voting rights outstanding.
retail_take: Routine buyback execution under announced programme; modest daily repurchase reflects steady capital management but has limited market significance.

headline: Purchase of Own Shares
brief: AdvancedAdvT continues share buyback, purchasing 10,000 shares at 160p on 20 July 2026.
summary: AdvancedAdvT Limited purchased 10,000 ordinary shares at 160 pence per share on 20 July 2026, continuing its announced buyback programme from March 2026. Cumulative purchases now total 5,263,100 shares held in treasury. Following the purchase, total voting rights number 131,862,706 shares, with total issued capital at 137,125,806 shares.
retail_take: Routine capital management execution; ongoing buyback programme supports shareholder returns but is non-material single transaction—typical for AIM-listed firms with board authorisation.

headline: Q3 Trading Update
brief: Compass Group reports Q3 organic revenue growth of 7.1%, maintains 2026 guidance for above 11% underlying operating profit growth.
summary: Compass Group delivered Q3 organic revenue growth of 7.1% (North America 7.5%, International 6.4%), with net new business wins of $4.3bn up 16% year-on-year and client retention at 96%. The company secured $2.4bn M&A spend year-to-date and expects capex around 3.5% of sales, slightly elevated at full year. Management reiterates 2026 guidance for above 11% underlying operating profit growth in constant currency, driven by ~7% organic revenue growth, ~2% from M&A, and ongoing margin progression.
retail_take: Market likely views this positively: consistent execution, accelerating new business wins, strong pipeline, and maintained FY26 guidance in a $360bn addressable market suggest sustained momentum. Sector diversification and first-time outsourcing gains reinforce growth runway.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Caledonia Investments repurchased 122,410 own shares at 380.50p average price for cancellation.
summary: Caledonia Investments PLC purchased 122,410 of its own ordinary shares on 20 July 2026 through Peel Hunt LLP at a weighted average price of 380.50 pence per share, within a range of 378.00p to 381.00p. The shares will be cancelled. Following this transaction, issued share capital comprises 512,210,320 ordinary shares with voting rights.
retail_take: Routine share buyback programme execution; demonstrates capital management but no material strategic signal for the market.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: HICL Infrastructure repurchases 750,000 shares at 135.02p, adding to treasury holdings.
summary: HICL Infrastructure PLC purchased 750,000 ordinary shares on 20 July 2026 through Investec Bank at a weighted average price of 135.02p, with trades ranging from 134.80p to 135.40p on the London Stock Exchange. The company now holds 168,045,988 shares in treasury. Total voting rights excluding treasury shares stand at 1,863,442,073, with the repurchased shares intended to be held as treasury stock.
retail_take: Routine share buyback supporting capital management and potentially earnings per share accretion; typical treasury stock activity with no material strategic implications for investors.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: HarbourVest Global Private Equity repurchased 8,000 shares at £32.98 for cancellation on 20 July 2026.
summary: HarbourVest Global Private Equity Limited (HVPE), a FTSE 250 investment company, purchased 8,000 ordinary shares on 20 July 2026 at an average price of £32.9753 per share for cancellation, exercising authority granted at its 15 July 2026 Annual General Meeting. Following this transaction, share capital reduced to 69,611,352 shares in issue.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution; modest scale and price near recent trading levels suggests ongoing capital management rather than distressed buying or market timing signal.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Science Group repurchases 15,000 shares at 585p as part of ongoing buyback programme.
summary: Science Group PLC purchased 15,000 of its own 1 pence ordinary shares on 20 July 2026 at 585 pence per share through Panmure Liberum Limited. The purchase represents 0.04% of voting rights, with shares held in treasury. Following the transaction, 40,672,593 ordinary shares remain in issue (excluding 5,513,281 treasury shares), and this figure serves as the denominator for FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules calculations.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution; no material impact signalled. Standard capital management activity with minimal voting rights impact.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: TRIG repurchases 265,000 shares at 74.55p as part of its buyback programme.
summary: The Renewables Infrastructure Group Limited purchased 265,000 ordinary shares on 20 July 2026 at a weighted average price of 74.5453p through Investec Bank plc, with prices ranging from 74.20p to 74.60p. The shares will be held as treasury stock. TRIG now holds 153.7 million treasury shares, with 2.33 billion voting shares outstanding excluding treasury holdings.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution under the announced programme; demonstrates capital allocation but signals no material new developments or market-moving catalysts.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares and Total Voting Rights
brief: India Capital Growth Fund repurchased 15,000 shares at 170p for treasury; voting shares now 65.1m.
summary: On 20 July 2026, India Capital Growth Fund Limited purchased 15,000 ordinary shares at 170p each for treasury. Following the transaction, issued share capital (excluding treasury) totals 65,102,624 shares with 47,399,549 held in treasury. Total voting shares now number 65,102,624. The fund manages £118.6m in assets across Indian mid and small-cap companies, with an average annual return of 12.23% since launch.
retail_take: Routine share buyback reducing share count marginally; typical capital management activity for closed-end funds seeking to support share price. No material significance.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Pantheon International repurchased 321,866 shares at 390-392p on 20 July, intending to cancel them.
summary: Pantheon International PLC purchased 321,866 ordinary shares on 20 July 2026 at prices between 390.00p and 392.00p, with a weighted average price of 390.99p per share, via J.P. Morgan Securities on the London Stock Exchange. The company intends to cancel these shares. Following the transaction, total ordinary shares in issue number 394,258,953, with no treasury shares held.
retail_take: Share buyback with cancellation slightly reduces share count and could modestly support EPS; signals management confidence in valuation, though buybacks are increasingly viewed as capital allocation neutral relative to dividends or investment.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: IWG purchases 153,361 shares at ~£1.96 average price as part of ongoing buyback programme.
summary: International Workplace Group plc bought 153,361 ordinary shares on 20 July 2026 at a volume-weighted average price of £1.9624 per share via Jefferies International Limited across multiple venues. Since the programme began 31 December 2025, the company has repurchased 40.16 million shares. Post-purchase, 954.1 million shares remain in issue (excluding treasury). Shares will either be cancelled or held in treasury.
retail_take: Routine capital management execution. Share buybacks are typically neutral to mildly positive—reducing share count aids EPS but signals no better M&A or organic investment opportunities.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: M.P. Evans repurchased 2,339 shares for cancellation on 20 July at an average price of 1,652.08p per share.
summary: M.P. Evans Group PLC purchased 2,339 of its own shares on 20 July 2026 for cancellation via broker Cavendish Capital Markets. Shares traded between 1,624p and 1,674p, with a volume-weighted average price of 1,652.08p. Following cancellation, total issued shares reduced to 52,157,565, with this figure representing voting rights for FCA Disclosure and Transparency Rules compliance.
retail_take: Routine share buyback demonstrating capital management and shareholder returns, though the modest volume suggests continued opportunistic purchases rather than material capital reallocation.

headline: IQE plc: Trading Update
brief: IQE lifts FY2026 revenue guidance to 30%+ growth on strong H1 performance, driven by AI and data centre demand.
summary: IQE reported H1 2026 revenue of at least £64m, exceeding expectations, with particularly strong demand for Indium Phosphide solutions used in optical photonics for data centres and AI. The company raised full-year 2026 revenue guidance to over 30% growth year-on-year, expecting low-teens £m adjusted EBITDA. Additional tailwinds from aerospace, defence, 3D sensing and wireless segments support continued momentum. IQE maintains a debt-free balance sheet with £41.6m cash as of 30 June 2026.
retail_take: Material upside beat with raised guidance and strong secular tailwinds (AI/data centres) suggest further momentum ahead; market likely to view positively given visibility into H2 and debt-free position enabling growth investment.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: M&C Saatchi repurchased 40,000 shares at 137.75p as part of its ongoing buyback programme.
summary: M&C Saatchi purchased 40,000 ordinary shares on 20 July 2026 at 137.75 pence per share via Panmure Liberum under its share buyback programme announced 9 March 2026. Following cancellation, the company will have 120,863,435 shares in issue with 485,970 held in treasury, reducing voting rights to 120,377,465. The transaction was executed at 08:40:36 UK time on AIM.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution demonstrating capital allocation discipline, though modest scale suggests measured approach; neither market-moving nor concerning for investors.

headline: Full Year 2026 Trading Update
brief: Kier Group delivers FY26 results at top end of expectations with £11.9bn order book providing strong visibility.
summary: Kier Group expects FY26 revenue and profit at the top end of market expectations, driven by strong momentum across Infrastructure (Water, Highways, Rail) and Construction (HMP Glasgow, M&E capability). Order book grew 8% to £11.9bn, representing over 90% of FY27 revenue already secured. Second-half new business totalled £2.5bn across Infrastructure (£1.5bn including Sizewell C, UK Fusion, South West Water) and Construction (£1.0bn across Education, Justice, Healthcare). Net cash position improved to £232m from £204m prior year.
retail_take: Strong operational momentum, robust order book providing FY27 visibility, and improved cash position suggest sustainable growth trajectory. Market likely to view positively given top-end guidance delivery and strategic alignment with UK Government infrastructure priorities.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Greencoat Renewables bought back 280,815 shares at €0.751-0.762 on 20 July as part of announced programme.
summary: Greencoat Renewables PLC executed a share buyback on 20 July 2026, purchasing 280,815 ordinary shares on Euronext Dublin at prices ranging from €0.7510 to €0.7620, with a volume-weighted average price of €0.7600. The shares will be cancelled. Following settlement, the company holds 200,000 treasury shares and has 1,083,105,394 shares in issue. This transaction forms part of the buyback programme announced on 5 March 2026.
retail_take: Routine capital management activity; share buybacks typically support shareholder returns and EPS, though impact depends on buyback programme scale relative to market cap.

headline: Unaudited results for the six months to 30/06/2026
brief: Arbuthnot Banking posts £11.0m H1 profit, raises interim dividend 9% to 24p despite lower interest rates.
summary: Arbuthnot Banking Group achieved £11.0m profit before tax for H1 2026 (vs £10.9m in H1 2025), with earnings per share rising to 49.9p from 42.5p. The interim dividend increased 2p to 24p per share. Customer deposits grew to £4.78bn (+4% H1, +8% YoY), customer loans reached £2.45bn (+9% H1, +6% YoY), and Funds Under Management surged to £3.0bn (+12% since year-end, +26% YoY). The group achieved its Future State 2 target of £10bn client balances two years ahead of schedule. Specialist lending exceeded £1bn.
retail_take: Positive read: strong organic growth across deposits, lending, and wealth management with dividend increase signals confidence despite lower interest rates. Capital ratios have declined but remain adequate, suggesting capacity for further growth.

headline: Contract Wins
brief: Billington secures three contracts worth £28m, mostly delivering in 2027 across education, energy and defence sectors.
summary: Billington Holdings has won three significant contracts totalling approximately £28 million. A North-West London education sector project worth £6 million, an energy-from-waste contract in Eastern England valued at £12 million for a repeat client, and a South-East England defence sector initial instruction expected to mature into a £10 million full order. All three projects are scheduled for 2027 delivery, with the defence contract subject to further development.
retail_take: Positive signal: diversified contract wins across resilient sectors with strong order book visibility into 2027, though defence contract remains conditional pending project maturation.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Currys repurchased 603,284 shares at 158.80p–162.00p between 14–20 July under its ongoing buyback programme.
summary: Currys PLC purchased 603,284 ordinary shares for cancellation in the week to 20 July 2026 at prices ranging from 158.80p to 162.00p, with a weighted average of 160.52p. The buyback was conducted via Panmure Liberum across four venues. Post-cancellation, total issued shares stand at 1,092,602,386. The programme was authorised at the September 2025 AGM and announced 2 July 2026.
retail_take: Routine capital return execution; modest weekly buyback activity within normal parameters suggests steady progress on shareholder distributions but carries no material strategic signal.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Airtel Africa purchased 841,759 shares at 325.6-340.4p in its ongoing buyback programme during 13-17 July.
summary: Airtel Africa executed share repurchases totalling 841,759 ordinary shares between 13-17 July 2026 via Barclays Capital Securities Limited at prices ranging from 325.6p to 340.4p, with a volume-weighted average price of 335.47p. Since programme commencement on 22 May 2026, the company has repurchased 13,706,328 shares at an average price of 339.29p. All purchased shares will be cancelled. The buyback was authorised by shareholders and previously announced.
retail_take: Routine capital management execution within disclosed buyback programme. Consistent purchasing at relatively stable price levels suggests orderly execution; no material implications for investor thesis.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Hollywood Bowl Group purchased 128,008 ordinary shares at 283-285p per share between 13-16 July for cancellation.
summary: Hollywood Bowl Group executed a share buyback programme between 13-20 July 2026, purchasing 128,008 ordinary shares of 1 pence each through Berenberg at weighted average prices ranging from 283.41p to 284.93p daily. Purchases occurred across four trading days on XLON with share prices trading between 283p and 285p. The company intends to cancel all purchased shares. Detailed transaction-level data covering approximately 100+ individual trades executed throughout each trading day was disclosed via RNS.
retail_take: Share buybacks typically signal management confidence in valuation but represent capital allocation away from growth investment; market reaction depends on cash position and shareholder return strategy context.

headline: Trading Update
brief: RentGuarantor reports record H1 2026 revenue of £3.39m (+250% YoY), achieves profitability with £250k adjusted net profit.
summary: RentGuarantor Holdings PLC achieved £3.39m revenue in H1 2026, up 250% year-on-year from £0.97m, with 3,703 completed contracts (+179% YoY) and 8,936 applications (+110% YoY). The company swung to adjusted net profit of £250k versus £367k loss in H1 2025, with adjusted EBITDA of £110k positive. Cash strengthened to £2.4m following £1.0m placing and repayment of all debt. Management expects FY2026 revenue materially above market expectations (£5.9-6.0m range) and adjusted profit before tax significantly ahead of £0.2-0.7m range.
retail_take: Strong operational momentum and profitability inflection driven by Renters' Rights Act tailwinds position RentGuarantor for accelerating growth, likely supporting near-term share performance amid positive guidance.

headline: Motzfeldt Field Programme Update
brief: Arkadian mobilises Motzfeldt field programme from 26 July with bulk sampling, hyperspectral surveying and environmental baseline studies underway.
summary: Arkadian's field team, led by Principal Geologist Dr Curtis Rooks, mobilises to Greenland from 26 July for the 2026 Motzfeldt programme. Activities include bulk sampling from the Aries JORC resource, hyperspectral imaging at Merino prospect via TheiaX, and environmental baseline studies by BioApp from 6 August. Flotation test work completed with assay results expected early August; historic drill core relogging planned for Q4 2026; satellite remote sensing data acquisition in progress.
retail_take: Execution of planned exploration programme on schedule with tangible near-term deliverables (assay results, imaging data) suggests operational competence; non-event unless results disappoint or delays emerge.

headline: ⁠Trading Update for the 42 Weeks to 18 July 2026
Total Voting Rights and Directors Dealings
brief: Cavendish PLC raises £281k through employee co-investment plan; 3.04m new shares issued at 9.25p.
summary: Cavendish completed its July Co-Investment Plan offer, with 17 employees subscribing £280,892 for 3,036,666 new ordinary shares at 9.25p per share on 20 July 2026. Additional shares (0.39-3.11% of issued capital) may be awarded based on share price targets ranging from 16.1p to 25.4p over three years. Directors participated: Co-CEOs Julian Morse and John Farrugia each acquired 324,324 shares; CFO Ben Procter acquired 216,216 shares. Farrugia separately exercised 2m options at 1p, retaining 945,405 shares after tax sales. Total voting rights now 390,161,764 shares.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of employee equity scheme and director dealings. Modest capital raise with dilution managed through EBT market purchases; non-event for broader shareholder base.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: IHG repurchased 1,000 shares at average $157.14 on 20 July 2026; shares to be cancelled.
summary: InterContinental Hotels Group purchased 1,000 ordinary shares on 20 July 2026 through Goldman Sachs International on the London Stock Exchange at prices ranging from $156.20 to $158.15, with a volume-weighted average price of $157.1420. The shares will be cancelled. Following this transaction, the company has 148.6 million shares in issue (excluding 5.4 million treasury shares). The repurchase was authorised by shareholders at the May 2025 AGM.
retail_take: Modest share buyback continues the company's capital return programme; de minimis scale suggests programme execution rather than material capital deployment signal.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Sabre Insurance completes share buyback, purchasing 227,270 shares at 182.60-185p for cancellation.
summary: Sabre Insurance Group PLC repurchased 227,270 ordinary shares between 14-20 July 2026 under its June 2026 buyback programme, with prices ranging from 182.60p to 185.00p. The average purchase price was 183.61p across four trading days. Following cancellation, total voting rights stand at 244,415,931 shares. All purchases were executed by Panmure Liberum across multiple venues including XLON, BATE, and CHIX.
retail_take: Routine execution of previously-announced buyback; modest scale suggests ongoing capital management rather than aggressive reduction. Non-event for market; consistent with shareholder authority granted in May.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Kingfisher repurchased 2.39m shares at £2.90–£2.97 per share in week of 16–17 July as part of £300m buyback programme.
summary: Kingfisher PLC purchased 2,390,232 ordinary shares for cancellation from Goldman Sachs International on 16–17 July 2026 at volume-weighted average prices of £2.9028 and £2.9704 respectively. Total programme purchases to date: 2.39m shares from GSI plus 26.65m previously from BNP Paribas under the first tranche. Trades executed across multiple venues (XLON, TRQX, CHIX, BATE, AQXE). Programme size: £300m announced 24 March 2026.
retail_take: Routine capital management; share buybacks typically neutral-to-mildly-positive signals, though execution at modest valuation levels (£2.90–£2.97) suggests no urgent pricing pressure. Non-event for most investors.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Plus500 repurchased 23,483 shares on 20 July 2026 at 4,044-4,196p per share under its ongoing buyback programme.
summary: Plus500 purchased 23,483 ordinary shares on 20 July 2026 at prices ranging from 4,044p to 4,196p, with a volume-weighted average price of 4,109.74p. The shares are held in treasury. Post-purchase, 68.96 million shares remain in issue (excluding treasury), while the company holds 45.93 million treasury shares. Total voting rights stand at 68,960,987.
retail_take: Routine capital allocation disclosure under the established buyback programme announced February 2026. Treasury share accumulation is non-material to valuation and signals modest shareholder return activity; no market-moving implications expected.

headline: Pre-close Trading Statement
brief: Midwich reports 10% adjusted profit growth in H1 2026 despite Middle East conflict headwinds; full-year results expected broadly in line with 2025.
summary: AIM-listed audiovisual distributor Midwich delivered H1 2026 adjusted PBT of approximately £10.6m, up 10% from £9.6m in H1 2025, on revenue of £640m (+3% reported, +2% constant currency). Excluding Middle East and exited businesses, profit grew 20%. Middle East conflict significantly impacted performance, particularly in live events; offsetting growth came from UK&I (revenue +10%), North America (+5%), though EMEA declined 5%. Adjusted net debt stood at £138m (2.3x leverage), expected to reduce to 2.0x by year-end. Board assumes challenging macroeconomic conditions will persist; full-year results anticipated broadly aligned with 2025 performance, contingent on sustained H1 momentum.
retail_take: Mixed read: organic growth and debt reduction trajectory are positive, but guidance is cautious and geopolitical exposure is material; market likely sees this as resilient but uninspiring given muted outlook.

headline: AGM Statement
brief: GBG holds AGM; Q1 revenue growth mid-single digit in core segments, momentum sustained in Identity and Location.
summary: GB Group reported Q1 FY2026 revenue growth in line with board expectations, with mid-single digit growth in core Identity and Location segments. Global Fraud Solutions experienced slower sales cycles versus strong prior-year comparisons. CEO Dev Dhiman highlighted strong EMEA Identity performance driven by GBG Go, the AI-powered identity platform, which delivered enhanced document and biometric verification capabilities plus deeper AI functionality in Q1. The board reaffirmed confidence in medium-term accelerated growth delivery.
retail_take: Solid but unexceptional trading update with core business momentum offset by weakness in smaller segment; reaffirmed medium-term guidance suggests management confidence, likely viewed as steady-state positive by markets.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares and Total Voting Rights
brief: Palace Capital completes share buyback programme, repurchasing 23,053 shares at 178.96p on 20 July 2026.
summary: Palace Capital PLC completed its share buyback programme announced 30 June 2026, purchasing 23,053 ordinary shares at a volume-weighted average price of 178.96 pence through Cavendish Capital Markets. The shares will be held in treasury. The company now has 19.5 million shares in issue with 3.6 million in treasury, reducing total voting rights to 15.9 million shares.
retail_take: Routine capital allocation announcement; share buybacks typically signal management confidence in valuation but have modest direct impact on retail investors. Standard market event.

headline: Half-year Financial Report
brief: MONY Group reports H1 2026 interim results with record revenue of £227.1m (+6% like-for-like) and Adjusted EBITDA of £75.5m.
summary: Mony Group delivered record H1 2026 revenue of £227.1m (up 6% like-for-like, 1% reported) and Adjusted EBITDA of £75.5m (up 3% like-for-like, 1% reported), marking five consecutive periods of growth. SuperSaveClub surpassed 2.5m members (19% of revenue) with stronger economics than non-members. The company launched AI-enabled products including Investments by MoneySuperMarket and SuperSaveClub Insurance. Basic EPS grew 4% to 8.9p; adjusted basic EPS rose 5% to 9.7p. Shareholders will receive £90m+ in returns including £25m buyback and progressive dividends (interim dividend +1% to 3.36p).
retail_take: Solid execution with revenue and earnings growth, member momentum, and meaningful product innovation offsetting headwinds in cashback. Market likely views positively given consistent delivery, capital returns, and AI-driven diversification beyond core comparison model.

headline: Condensed Consolidated F.Statements June 30
brief: CIB Egypt posts H1 2026 interim results showing net profit growth and asset expansion.
summary: Commercial International Bank (Egypt) reported H1 2026 net profit of EGP 39.3bn, up 17.9% YoY from EGP 33.3bn. Net interest income grew 18.5% to EGP 60.8bn. Total assets increased 17.2% to EGP 1.69tn, with customer deposits rising 17.8% to EGP 1.31tn. Loans and advances grew 18.3%. Basic earnings per share climbed to 10.23 from 8.82. Cost-to-income improved. Liquidity and capital positions remain solid with EGP 226.3bn cash equivalents.
retail_take: Solid double-digit earnings and asset growth with margin expansion signals healthy underlying business; moderate dividend payout (EGP 29bn) and reserve transfers support capital strength—likely read as positive but not exceptional given emerging market banking context.

headline: Return of Capital Update
brief: Helical completes £12m B Share redemption; proceeds to shareholders by 4 August.
summary: Helical PLC has completed redemption of B Shares issued under its May 2026 capital return scheme, returning approximately £12 million to shareholders. Redemption proceeds will be dispatched on 4 August 2026. Following the B Share Scheme and Share Consolidation, the company now has 117.5 million ordinary shares issued (116.5 million voting rights after treasury shares), each valued at 1.05 pence.
retail_take: Routine capital return completion; shareholder distributions now underway. Non-event for market as scheme was previously approved and announced.

headline: Condensed Separate Financial Statements June 2026
brief: CIB Egypt reports H1 2026 interim results with net profit of EGP 39.2m, up 17.5% YoY despite challenging environment.
summary: Commercial International Bank (Egypt) SAE reported H1 2026 net profit of EGP 39.2m versus EGP 33.4m in H1 2025, representing 17.5% growth. Net interest income rose to EGP 60.5m from EGP 51.2m YoY. Total assets grew 17.1% to EGP 1.68bn as of June 30, 2026. Loans and advances to customers increased to EGP 591.3m. The bank paid dividends of EGP 29m during the period. EPS improved to 10.35 (basic) from 8.82 in the prior year period.
retail_take: Solid earnings growth and asset expansion suggest resilient operational performance in a challenging Egyptian macroeconomic backdrop. Margin improvement and controlled costs are encouraging, though elevated tax burden and FX headwinds require monitoring.

headline: Trading Statement
brief: Wickes reports 2.3% Q2 revenue growth with positive LFL trends across both Retail and Design & Installation divisions.
summary: Wickes Group delivered 2.3% revenue growth in Q2 2026 (quarter ending 27 June), with Group revenue of £483m. Retail revenue reached £366m with 1.8% growth and 0.6% LFL growth; Design & Installation revenue was £117m with 3.8% growth and 1.8% LFL growth. TradePro membership grew 9% to 671,000, while Click & Collect and Home Delivery sales jumped 7%. The company maintained net cash of £152m, completed £10m share buybacks, and remains comfortable with consensus 2026 adjusted PBT forecasts of £55.4m mean.
retail_take: Market likely views this positively as Wickes demonstrates resilience with volume-driven growth, strong TradePro momentum, and market share gains despite deflationary pricing and consumer uncertainty, while maintaining robust balance sheet and meeting guidance.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Molten Ventures VCT completes £2.56m share buyback, cancelling 6.67m shares (2.11% of capital).
summary: On 20 July 2026, Molten Ventures VCT PLC purchased 6,671,869 of its own Ordinary Shares at 38.29 pence per share for cancellation, representing approximately 2.11% of issued share capital. The buyback, conducted via broker Panmure Liberum, cost approximately £2.56m. Following cancellation, the Company now has 309,733,674 Ordinary Shares in issue with no treasury shares held.
retail_take: Routine capital management; share buybacks can signal confidence but are typically neutral unless coupled with earnings guidance or strategic announcements.

headline: Half-year Financial Report
brief: Brunner Investment Trust reports 5.6% NAV total return for H1 2026, lagging 12.0% benchmark due to narrow AI-driven market leadership.
summary: NAV per share rose to 1,640.1p (fair value basis) from 1,565.8p, delivering 5.6% total return versus 12.0% benchmark. Share price total return reached 8.1%. EPS increased 13.9% to 19.7p; dividend per share rose 8.0% to 13.5p with full-year forecast of 27.0p, maintaining 54-year consecutive increase record. The trust underperformed due to deliberate diversification strategy avoiding concentrated AI exposure, holding positions in TSMC, ASML and Microchip while missing momentum-driven tech rallies. Discount to NAV averaged 8.8% over period; 156,470 shares repurchased for £2.2m.
retail_take: Underperformance versus benchmark will frustrate some investors, but strong dividend growth (8%), solid absolute returns, and transparent valuation-disciplined philosophy appeal to long-term income-focused shareholders skeptical of AI valuation extremes.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: VinaCapital Vietnam Opportunity Fund repurchased 35,228 shares at GBP 4.34 per share on 20 July 2026.
summary: VinaCapital Vietnam Opportunity Fund Limited repurchased 35,228 USD-denominated ordinary shares on 20 July 2026 at GBP 4.335304 per share, to be held as treasury shares. Total treasury shares now stand at 6,640,142, with 120,439,381 shares in issue after treasury deduction. Total voting rights in the company are 120,439,381.
retail_take: Routine share buyback programme execution; treasury share accumulation suggests capital management but minimal direct impact on shareholders without broader context on buyback rationale or scale.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Babcock International repurchased 14,441 shares on 20 July 2026 at average price of 1037.51p as part of ongoing buyback programme.
summary: Babcock International Group purchased 14,441 ordinary shares on 20 July 2026 via J.P. Morgan Securities plc at prices ranging from 1030.0p to 1043.5p, with volume-weighted average of 1037.51p. Since 1 July 2026, the company has purchased 872,822 shares for £8.8m. Following this transaction, treasury holdings total 13.45m shares with 492.15m shares remaining in issue.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution at mid-range valuations; demonstrates capital allocation flexibility but provides limited insight into management sentiment on valuation.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: Three C&C Group executives purchased shares via employee Share Incentive Plan on 15 July at £0.962 per share.
summary: Three PDMRs at C&C Group plc purchased ordinary shares through the company's Share Incentive Plan on 15 July 2026 at £0.962 per share. Cara Chambers (Chief Marketing Officer) acquired 156 shares (78 Partnership Shares + 78 Matching Shares) for £150.07; Karen Bates (Chief People Officer) and Paul Graham (Chief Commercial Officer) each acquired 260 shares (130 Partnership Shares + 130 Matching Shares) for £250.12. The company matched employee contributions as per SIP terms.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of insider share purchases through standard employee scheme with company matching; no material implications for share price or strategy.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: JSG repurchased 1.99m shares at 142-145p in week to 17 July as part of announced buyback; shares to be cancelled.
summary: Johnson Service Group PLC purchased 1,992,130 ordinary shares between 13-17 July 2026 via Investec Bank plc under its buyback programme announced 7 May 2026. Weighted average prices ranged from 142.59p (13 July) to 145.45p (17 July), with daily volumes between 239,594 and 573,492 shares. Total consideration approximately £2.84m. Shares will be cancelled, reducing issued share count and enhancing EPS for remaining shareholders.
retail_take: Routine capital allocation; buybacks at steady prices signal management confidence in valuation. Modest accretive impact on EPS through share count reduction. No material positive or negative signal.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Fever-Tree purchased 249,179 own shares at 767–820p over 13–17 July 2026 for cancellation.
summary: Fever-Tree Drinks PLC executed a share buyback programme authorised at the June 2025 AGM, purchasing 249,179 ordinary shares between 13–17 July 2026 across XLON and AIMX venues. Total acquisition cost approximately £19.3m at volume-weighted average prices ranging 765.0p–815.2p daily. Shares will be cancelled, reducing issued capital. Buyback reflects capital return strategy and potential confidence in valuation.
retail_take: Routine capital management action. Share buybacks can support EPS through reduction in share count but may signal management sees stock as undervalued or excess cash. No major news event; market typically neutral unless buyback surprise or concurrent guidance issued.

headline: Contract
brief: Galliford Try appointed to £9.5bn CPC Construction and Capital Works framework across all five regional lots.
summary: Galliford Try has secured appointment to the Crescent Purchasing Consortium (CPC) Construction and Capital Works framework worth £9.5 billion, covering all five regional lots across England, Wales and Scotland for up to eight years. The framework encompasses new-build and refurbishment projects across education, healthcare and civic infrastructure sectors. CEO Bill Hocking highlighted the appointment reflects the group's position as a leading education sector contractor.
retail_take: Material contract win across major public sector framework demonstrates sustained demand for Galliford Try's services and provides visible medium-term revenue visibility, likely supporting investor sentiment.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Gaming Realms completes share buyback, purchasing 124,722 shares at 30.50-31.00p per share.
summary: Gaming Realms PLC purchased 124,722 ordinary shares on 20 July 2026 through Peel Hunt at prices ranging from 30.50p to 31.00p, with a volume-weighted average price of 30.79p. The shares will be held in treasury. Following this purchase, the company holds 24,364,287 treasury shares and has 271,901,727 shares in issue, representing total voting rights.
retail_take: Routine capital management disclosure executing an announced buyback programme. No material implications for investors beyond standard share count adjustments.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Venture Life repurchases 200,000 shares at 70.40p each under its buyback programme.
summary: Venture Life Group PLC purchased 200,000 ordinary shares on 20 July 2026 at a volume-weighted average price of 70.40 pence per share through Cavendish Capital Markets, in line with its September 2025 buyback programme. The shares will be held as treasury shares. Post-purchase, the company has 128.9m shares in issue (including 7.8m treasury shares) and 121.1m voting rights.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution under existing programme; suggests management confidence but represents minor capital allocation activity with no material earnings impact.

headline: Latin-America Updates
brief: Sintana Energy reports exploration updates in Uruguay and Argentina with extended timelines and new tender process.
summary: Sintana's AREA OFF-1 offshore Uruguay block received a one-year suspension of its initial exploration subperiod, now expiring 23 August 2027, to allow completion of 3D seismic acquisition and processing delayed by environmental authorisation. In Argentina, Decree 590/2026 was published instructing the National Secretariat of Energy to call an International Public Tender for the North Argentine Basin permit (CAN-200), following Challenger Energy Group's February 2025 expression of interest.
retail_take: Timeline extensions and new tender processes signal operational delays rather than strategic wins; exploration progress is modest, reducing near-term catalyst value for investors seeking near-term catalysts.

headline: Exercise of Warrants
brief: Galantas Gold exercises 475,000 warrants at C$0.12, raising capital for Chilean gold projects.
summary: Galantas Gold Corporation exercised warrants over 475,000 ordinary shares at C$0.12 per share across two dates in mid-July 2026, generating approximately C$57,000 in proceeds. The new shares will be admitted to AIM trading on or around 23 July 2026, bringing total issued share capital to 831.4 million shares. The company is developing the Indiana and Andacollo Gold Projects in Chile.
retail_take: Routine warrant exercise generating modest capital; modest dilution with minimal cash impact relative to company size; signals continued development funding for Chilean assets.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Gamma Communications repurchased 13,292 shares at 937-951.5p on 20 July as part of its ongoing buyback programme announced in January.
summary: Gamma Communications purchased 13,292 ordinary shares on 20 July 2026 at prices ranging from 937p to 951.5p per share, with a weighted average price of 943.70p, via Investec Bank. These shares will be cancelled. To date, 2,747,063 shares have been repurchased under the programme. Post-settlement, 91,017,288 shares remain in issue with 89,503,266 voting shares outstanding (excluding treasury holdings of 1,514,022 shares).
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution within announced programme; minor capital allocation signalling but no material market catalyst. Ongoing in offer period context.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: GlobalData repurchased 100,000 shares at 73.40-75.50p as part of its buyback programme launched 23 June 2026.
summary: GlobalData PLC purchased 100,000 ordinary shares on 20 July 2026 at a weighted average price of 74.9842p through Investec Bank plc, with prices ranging from 73.40p to 75.50p. The shares will be cancelled. Following the buyback, 745,609,786 ordinary shares remain in issue with no treasury shares held. This represents the company's capital management activity under its announced buyback programme.
retail_take: Routine capital management disclosure; share buybacks are typically viewed neutrally by markets as they reduce share count but use cash that could fund growth or dividends—sentiment depends on execution price relative to fair value.

headline: Pershing Square Holdings, Ltd. Announces Appointment of Ranjani Kearsley as Independent Non-Executive Director
brief: Pershing Square Holdings appoints Ranjani Kearsley as independent non-executive director, effective July 20, 2026.
summary: Ranjani Kearsley, who brings over 25 years of asset management experience across regulated firms in the US, Europe, and Asia, has been appointed as an independent non-executive director to PSH's Board. She currently holds board positions at Twelve Securis Holdings, Strategic Investment Management Group, and Longview Partners entities, and previously held senior roles at Fidelity International and Eight Roads. The appointment follows an extensive search process and is intended to enhance the Board's governance, strategy, and global perspective.
retail_take: Routine governance strengthening; experienced independent director appointment suggests solid board composition refresh with no material implications for investors.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Pershing Square Holdings repurchased 35,400 shares on 20 July at average price of $50.89.
summary: PSH purchased 35,400 public shares through Jefferies on 20 July 2026 at an average price of $50.89 USD. The buyback comprised 24,132 shares on LSE (PSH ticker) at 3,791 pence average and 11,268 shares (PSHD ticker) at $50.92 average. NAV per share was $77.06 as of 14 July. Post-buyback, PSH has 173.75m shares outstanding with 37.2m held in treasury.
retail_take: Share buyback at discount to NAV ($50.89 vs $77.06) is accretive to remaining shareholders; routine capital management activity with no material implications.

headline: Dividend Declaration
brief: PSH confirms Q3 2026 quarterly dividend of $0.1837 per share, payable 18 September 2026.
summary: Pershing Square Holdings confirms its third quarter 2026 dividend of $0.1837 per Public Share, with record date 14 August 2026 and payment date 18 September 2026. Shareholders can reinvest via DRIP or elect GBP payment by 28 August 2026. The company notes dividend payment remains subject to solvency requirements and debt-to-capitalisation limits, and that future dividends are not guaranteed despite current intentions to pay quarterly.
retail_take: Routine dividend confirmation maintaining PSH's quarterly payout policy; the cautionary language on future dividends reflects standard fund governance but suggests no material change in distribution outlook.

headline: Share Repurchase Program - Weekly Report
brief: Sunbelt Rentals repurchased 35,000 shares in week of 13-17 July at average price $73.63, reducing share count.
summary: Sunbelt Rentals purchased 35,000 shares across five trading days (13-17 July 2026) under its $1.5bn repurchase program. Daily volumes: 7,000 shares each day; prices ranged $72.86–$74.03, with weighted average $73.63. Treasury shares now total 4,364,501; outstanding shares reduced to 409,599,584. All purchases executed on NYSE.
retail_take: Routine capital management execution within existing authorization; modest weekly buyback pace ($2.58m notional); neither materially accretive nor dilutive signal at these valuations—neutral market read.

headline: Total Voting Rights
brief: BlackRock American Income Trust updates voting rights denominator to 61.46m shares as at 21 July 2026.
summary: BlackRock American Income Trust plc notified the market of its issued share capital as at 21 July 2026, consisting of 61,460,138 Ordinary Shares carrying one vote per share (with 33,901,167 shares held in treasury). The company has designated 61,460,138 as the denominator for shareholding calculations under FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules (DTR 5.6.1), which shareholders must use to determine notification obligations for interests in the company.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure setting the voting share denominator; no material implications for shareholders or investment thesis.

headline: NBPE Announces Transaction in Own Shares
brief: NBPE repurchases and cancels 120,000 Class A shares at £14.24-£14.36 per share on LSE.
summary: Neuberger Private Equity Partners Limited executed a share buyback on 20 July 2026, purchasing 120,000 Class A shares at prices between £14.24 and £14.36 per share under shareholder authority granted 11 June 2026. All repurchased shares will be cancelled. Following cancellation, outstanding Class A shares total 40,614,149, with an additional 3,150,408 shares held in treasury. The buyback was conducted via Jefferies International Limited.
retail_take: Share cancellation reduces the share count, which can be accretive to earnings per share; routine capital management indicating management confidence in valuation and deployment of cash.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Societe Generale reduces ICG holding from 9.51% to 6.43% through share disposal and CFD unwinding.
summary: Societe Generale's voting rights in Intermediate Capital Group fell below the 9% threshold, dropping from 9.51% to 6.43% between 17-20 July 2026. The reduction comprised 17.92m direct shares (6.35%) and 230k voting rights via cash-settled CFDs expiring between December 2027 and May 2029 (0.08%). The threshold crossing was notified to the issuer on 20 July.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of major shareholding change. Societe Generale's partial exit removes a significant stakeholder but is non-news-driving given pre-announced nature of threshold crossings and lack of strategic commentary.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: TotalEnergies repurchased 1.46m shares for €103m during July 13-17, 2026 at average €70.38/share.
summary: TotalEnergies executed share buybacks across five trading days totaling 1,462,893 shares for €102.95m under shareholder authorization from May 2026. Daily volumes ranged from 178,638 to 186,263 shares traded across multiple venues (XPAR, CEUX, TQEX, AQEU), with weighted average prices between €69.26-€71.48 per share. The program demonstrates capital deployment during the period and reflects management confidence in valuation.
retail_take: Routine capital management disclosure. Share buybacks are typically neutral-to-mildly positive signals reflecting confidence but are subordinate to cash returns and investment priorities for most investors.

company: BlackRock Greater Europe Investment Trust Plc
headline: Total Voting Rights
brief: BlackRock Greater Europe Investment Trust updates voting rights denominator to 90.4m shares as at 21 July 2026.
summary: BlackRock Greater Europe Investment Trust plc disclosed that as of 21 July 2026, its issued capital comprises 90,373,001 ordinary shares of £0.001 each, excluding 27,555,937 shares held in treasury. The company notified the market that shareholders must use the 90,373,001 figure as the denominator for calculating disclosure thresholds under FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules 5.6.1.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure establishing the voting rights denominator for share ownership notification purposes; no material implications for investors.

headline: Total Voting Rights
brief: BlackRock Smaller Companies Trust plc discloses total voting rights denominator for shareholder notification purposes.
summary: As of 21 July 2026, BlackRock Smaller Companies Trust plc had 244,603,950 ordinary shares of £0.05 each in issued capital, excluding 54,088,655 shares held in treasury. This figure of 244,603,950 shares is the denominator shareholders must use to calculate whether they are required to notify their interests or changes to interests under FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules 5.6.1.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure with no material implications; standard transparency filing for calculating significant shareholding thresholds.

headline: Advisory Panel Appointments and Issue of Equity Further to Exercise of Warrants
